envelope_assembly: Project-specific; durable waterfront/institutional envelope where applicable

sources.title: Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Zoos and Nature Center
AZA resources (used conceptually for animal care, veterinary and operational separation)
Smithsonian exhibition planning guidance (used only for interpretive/exhibit logic)

small.name: Nature Center / Small Zoological Park
small.notes: Interpretive center, classrooms, small support facilities

medium.name: Regional Zoo Campus
medium.notes: Visitor center, exhibits, keeper support, clinic

large.name: Major Urban Zoo
large.notes: Distributed visitor/support buildings across large site

space_library.name: Entry / Ticketing / Orientation
notes: Main public threshold; queueing and stroller management often important.

space_library.name: Interpretive Exhibit Hall / Visitor Building
notes: Indoor exhibit component; site-wide outdoor exhibits handled at campus-planning level.

space_library.name: Education Classroom / Camp Room
notes: Supports school groups, camps, and interpretation.

space_library.name: Concessions Support / Pantry
notes: Populate full kitchen later if operator model requires.

space_library.name: Animal Holding / Night House
notes: Species-specific holding geometry intentionally omitted until collection plan is defined.

space_library.name: Keeper Prep / Feed / Staff Support
notes: Staff changing, prep, and secure operations support.

space_library.name: Veterinary / Quarantine
notes: Basic clinic/quarantine placeholder only; species mix determines final scope.

space_library.name: Service Yard / Deliveries
notes: Keep service traffic separate from visitors.

space_library.name: Public Restrooms
notes: Campus models may distribute these across site.
